message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Introduction</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The COVID-19 is caused by the virus known as sever acute respiratory syndrome corona virus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) having the common symptoms such as Flue, fever, dry cough and shortness of breath. The first case was reported in WUHAN city china in December 2019 and it spread to the whole world, WHO declared as world pandemic on 11
                    <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                    march 2020.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>SIR Epidemiological Model</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The first case in Pakistan was confirmed on 26
                    <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                    Feb 2020 as by the 8
                    <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                    April 2020 the total no of confirmed cases 4187 with 58 deaths and 467 recoveries throughout the country. The upcoming situation of the COVID-19 in Pakistan is forecasted by using SIR epidemiological, which is one of the mathematical derivative models with great accuracy rate prediction used for infectious disease. This model was introduced in the early 20
                    <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                    century.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Pakistan is will be having a heavy burden of patients 80000 plus infected patients 45000 recoveries 10000 hospitalized 3000 ICU and 800 plus deaths in the next 20 days. A complete lock down, social distancing and imposing curfew to keep every person at home can save Pakistan from a very huge number 1000000 infected patients with huge number of causalities with next 2 months.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: The Epidemiology of COVID-19 and applying Non Pharmaceutical interventions by using the Susceptible, Infectious Recovered epidemiological Model in Pakistan

reference.article-title: Pathological findings of COVID-19 associated with acute respiratory distress syndrome

reference.unstructured: Guan, W. , et al., China Medical Treatment Expert Group for Covid-19. Clinical characteristics of coronavirus disease, 2019.

reference.article-title: A contribution to the mathematical theory of epidemics
reference.journal-title: Proceedings of the royal society of london. Series A, Containing papers of a mathematical and physical character

reference.unstructured: Chatterjee, K. , et al., Healthcare impact of COVID-19 epidemic in India: A stochastic mathematical model. Medical Journal Armed Forces India, 2020.
Naresh Dhanwant, J. and V. Ramanathan , Forecasting COVID 19 growth in India using Susceptible-Infected-Recovered (SIR) model. arXiv, 2020: p. arXiv: 2004.00696.
Peng, L. , et al., Epidemic analysis of COVID-19 in China by dynamical modeling. arXiv preprint arXiv:2002.06563, 2020.
Simha, A. , R.V. Prasad , and S. Narayana , A simple Stochastic SIR model for COVID 19 Infection Dynamics for Karnataka: Learning from Europe. arXiv preprint arXiv:2003.11920, 2020.
Khalid, F. and A.N. Abbasi , Challenges faced by Pakistani healthcare system: Clinician’s perspective. 2018.
Lai, S. , et al., Effect of non-pharmaceutical interventions for containing the COVID-19 outbreak: an observational and modelling study. medRxiv, 2020.
Krr, G. and F. Casella , Non-Pharmaceutical Interventions (NPIs) to Reduce COVID-19 Mortality. Available at SSRN 3560688, 2020.
